{{Quote|The L-Eight-L-Nine unit is completely lost, Baron. And so is the hundred thousand credits you wagered on it to win.|Report to [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]] after L8-L9 was defeated by [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]]|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />}}
'''L8-L9''' was a prototype [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] built by [[Tagge Company/Legends|TaggeCo]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The droid was created in the utmost secrecy, and was part of a ploy by [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]] to gain favor in the sight of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].

To test the L8-L9, Orman Tagge arranged the droid to fight in the [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]] arenas to show off its abilities. Unfortunately for L8-L9 and Tagge, [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] bested the robot, therefore shutting down any further production of the combat droid L8-L9 was based on.

However, the legacy of the project lived on through the [[Z-X3 experimental droid trooper]], also worked on by TaggeCo. Eventually, while both products failed, they led to the famed [[Dark Trooper/Legends|Dark trooper]] project.

==Description==
[[File:Youngormantagge.jpg|thumb|left|180px|[[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]] with L8-L9.]]
The droid was equipped with advanced [[Servomotor/Legends|servomotors]] and [[equilibrium center]]s. These additions helped to give the droid increased agility and maneuverability when compared to most battle droids in use during the Clone Wars. Mounted inside the droid's arms were built-in [[Plasma cannon/Legends|plasma cannons]] and [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]],<ref name="L8-L9 Databank entry" /> which proved effective against most combatants.<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars" />

The helmet of the combat droid was purposfully modeled after that of a clone trooper. TaggeCo felt that this "nod" to the prospective buyers would help persuade the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] to throw their support to the project. An enhanced sensor globe sat in the upermost portion of the helmet. The "fingers" located at the tips of the blast nozzles were of little use in manipulating objects, but, when spinning rapidly as they had been designed to do, could cut through solid [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />

==History==
===Design===
[[File:L8L9.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The red L8-L9 in the [[The Cauldron|Rattatakian arena]].]]
Orman Tagge began sketching out plans for L8-L9 almost immediately after the blockade and [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], as an army for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] to use against the droid forces of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], TaggeCo frantically moved the units from design to production, through a secret division.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />

Tagge intended to replace infantry units in environments that were deemed unsafe, even for the Republic's expendable [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. Eventually, he hoped that the droid units would phase out the need for the troopers.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />

The product was considered to be top secret for several reasons,<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> most notably the restrictions placed on battle droid development following the invasion of Naboo. TaggeCo also wanted to protect the design from competing companies.<ref name="L8-L9 Databank entry" />

As production continued, the L8-L9 became a pet project for Orman Tagge,<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> who eventually set the combat droid as a foothold to gain favor in the sight of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine.<ref name="L8-L9 Databank entry" />

===Testing the new droid===
Before making the creation of the L8-L9 public, Tagge wanted to test it under the most extreme conditions available.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> Scouts from the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] reported that the back-water [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]] and its barbaric arena death matches would be excellent training grounds.<ref name="L8-L9 Databank entry" /> Tagge ordered that L8-L9 was to be tested there and entered into one of the arena battles. The young man was so confident in the combat droid's abilities that he bet 100,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on his victory.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />

Unfortunately for Tagge, the prototype L8-L9 was doomed due to the Dark Jedi Asajj Ventress. During the battle between the gladiators, L8-L9 identified Asajj as the greatest threat, and attacked her.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> The resulting spread of fire and plasma took out many other contenders, but was unable to defeat Ventress, who held L8-L9 in a [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force grip]] and destroyed him by smashing him against the ceiling.<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars" />

===Legacy of L8-L9===
[[File:L8L9rattatak.jpg|thumb|right|200px|L8-L9 in the [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatakian]] arena.]]
The aftermath of the battle caused TaggeCo to halt production of any further combat droids based on L8-L9. However, Tagge continued to work on his idea of an automated trooper, and with the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and help from [[General/Legends|General]] [[Rom Mohc]], TaggeCo began work on a new droid trooper project. This [[Z-X3 experimental droid trooper|new unit]] would supplement [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in later battles. Eventually, both of these projects would be considered and improved on in the later [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] projects.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" />

==Behind the scenes==
L8-L9 was first sighted in [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 6]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', where the droid was Asajj Ventress's main opponent.<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars" />

L8-L9's original color scheme was a slate blue;<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> however, when entered into the Rattatakian arena, the paint color was changed to red.<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars" />
